pr.title: Upgrade Pygments to `2.15.0` to address vulnerability
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the repository Python dependency constraint for Pygments in requirements.txt from an exact pin at 2.14.0 to a minimum version of 2.15.0 to address a vulnerability. The PR was a small dependency-maintenance/security fix with no review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Changed the Pygments requirement in requirements.txt from `Pygments==2.14.0` to `Pygments>=2.15.0`.
No C source, headers, decompilation logic, or matching code was changed in the provided diff.
The PR title states the motivation was addressing a vulnerability in the older Pygments version.
smart_moves: Used a minimal one-line dependency change, reducing the risk of unrelated tooling churn.
Moved the Pygments constraint to `>=2.15.0`, ensuring installations avoid the vulnerable 2.14.0 version while allowing newer patched releases.
decomp_lessons: Security updates to Python tooling dependencies can be isolated in requirements.txt without touching decompilation source files.

For infrastructure dependencies used by the project, a minimum-version constraint can be preferable to an exact vulnerable pin when the goal is to ensure all installs receive at least the patched version.
This PR provides no evidence about symbol naming, struct layout, headers, or assembly matching tactics.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that update requirements.txt for security vulnerabilities.
Find dependency updates that changed exact pins to minimum-version constraints.
Find PRs touching Python tooling dependencies used by the decompilation workflow.
